top.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
top.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

top.title: Israeli demolitions levelling towns in south Lebanon, satellite images show
top.summary: BBC Verify analysis found more than 1,400 buildings had been destroyed since 2 March.

top.title: Frank Gardner: What is China's role in the Iran war?
top.summary: BBC Security Correspondent Frank Gardner explains how the world's second-largest economy fits into the Gulf conflict.

top.title: Social media leaders called to Downing Street over children's safety
top.summary: Top executives from firms such as Meta and YouTube will be asked what they are doing to protect children.

top.title: Booking.com customers warned of 'reservation hijacking' after hack
top.summary: The travel platform said it had changed Pins to protect customers but would not say how many were affected.

top.title: Google to punish sites that trap people in with back button tricks
top.summary: The tech giant said it will punish sites that block back button navigation from June.
